entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Make fitness-building routines a habit.

This topic is included in the following course(s): PE: Grades 4-6, PE: Grades 7-12

PE: Grades 4-6
Lessons specifically created for Grades 1-6. We recommend you make sure to include some sort of physical activity as breaks throughout your school day. If you already have sports or similar activities as a regular part of your life rhythms, these lessons are optional.

PE: Grades 7-12
Lessons specifically created for Grades 7-12. We do recommend you prioritize the Historical Dance lessons as these are less likely to be obtained elsewhere and also give depth and richness to student's understanding of the time period. We recommend you make sure to include some sort of physical activity as breaks throughout your school day. If you already have sports or similar activities as a regular part of your life rhythms, these lessons are optional.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Students may also choose to take a local Pilates or Yoga class.

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

PE: Grades 4-6
☐ You may need to gather supplies for the activities, so plan accordingly.

Free Play/ Circuits
☐ You will want to figure out the circuit.

supplies.title: Dumbbell Hand Weight Sets of 2 (1-3 pounds)

blocks.text: Mason emphasized that being physically fit is just as important as being mentally and spiritually fit. She used a combination of Swedish Drill (calisthenic exercises done to music or military-style commands), playground games, sports, dance, free play, and afternoon walks to make sure students had time for vigorous exercise every single day.
Teach students the new song, dance, or sport. Tutorials are often provided.
Play your new game, practice your new dance, sing your new rhyme, etc.!

lessons.title: 20m Free Play/ Circuits - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: 303 Tween-approved Exercises + Active Games, a pair of light weights or cans

PREP: Go through the exercises with your student so they know what to do and can use the correct form. 

➜ NOTE
There is one assigned circuit routine per week. One week will be upper body and the next week will be lower body. The same circuits will be repeated for about 3 weeks. Feel free to do the circuits more than once a week. They can be done every day if you are alternating muscles you are using.  Feel free to change up the exercises or add in more. If you have a group you can all do the same exercises together or have stations that everyone goes to and then rotates to the next station. You can also put a cardio exercise in between the station such as jump roping or running in place or jumping on a small trampoline. 

➜ EXERCISE  
Circuit 1: Upper Body Training

1\. Warm-Up Activity (5 min)
(Pick an activity from p.24-25)

2\. Stretching Exercises (5 min)
(Pick 3 stretches from p.26-31)

3\. Circuit Exercises (Do this set 3 times through)
• Crossover Abs (#54) 5x each side
• Ball Twists (#55) 5x each side
• Close the Door (#90) 10x
• Push and touch (#91) 10x
• Bent -Over Circular Rows (10x)

4\. Add some kind of cardio exercise or sports game at some point throughout the day. There are many ideas in the book.

lessons.title: 20m Free Play/ Circuits - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
➜ PLAY
Free Play

lessons.title: 20m Free Play/ Circuits - Lesson 3
lessons.body:

1\. Warm-Up Activity (5 min)
(Pick an activity from p.24-25)

2\. Stretching Exercises (5 min)
(Pick 3 stretches from p.26-31)

3\. Circuit 2: Lower Body Training
• Plank with Torso Rotation (#56) 5x each side
• Ball Toss (#57) 5x
• Front Kicks (#120) 5x
• Squats (#122) 5x
• Rear-Leg Lifts (124) 10x each side

4\. Add some kind of cardio exercise or sports game at some point throughout the day. There are many ideas in the book.

(Pick 3 stretches from p.26-31)

3\. Circuit Exercises (Do this set 3 times through)
• Ball Toss (#57) 5x
• Crunches with Knee Rolls (#58) 5x
• Bent-Over Circular Rows (#92) 10x 
• Crisscross Reverse Flies (#93) 5x
• Overhead Triceps (#94) Repeat for at least one minute

⍞ Materials: 303 Tween-approved Exercises + Active Games, a pair of light weights or cans, a ball for each child

➜ EXERCISE  
Circuit 3: Upper Body Training

(Pick an activity from p.24-25)

2\. Stretching Exercises (5 min)
(Pick 3 stretches from p.26-31)

3\. Circuit Exercises (Do this set 3 times through)
• Back-Twist Extensions (#61) hold for 5 breaths and then repeat the exercises with other side.
• Leg-Up Crunches (#62) 5x
• Biceps Curls (#96) 5x each side
• Supine Triceps Press (#97) hold for two seconds, 10x each side
• Shoulder/Back Squeeze (#98) hold for two seconds, 5x each side

Free Play

lessons.title: 20m Free Play/ Circuits - Lesson 61
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: 303 Tween-approved Exercises + Active Games, a small block

➜ EXERCISE  
Circuit 7: Upper Body Training

1\. Warm-Up Activity (5 min)
(Pick an activity from p.24-25)

2\. Stretching Exercises (5 min)
(Pick 3 stretches from p.26-31)

3\. Circuit Exercises (Do this set 3 times through)
• Lifted Reverse Crunches (#73) 5x
• Double-Leg Lifts (#74) 5x
• The Pinch (#105) hold for 15 sec, 5x
• The Lift (#106) Hold for 15 sec, 5x
• Crisscross Flies (#107) 5x

lessons.title: 20m Free Play/ Circuits - Lesson 62
lessons.body:
➜ PLAY
Free Play

lessons.title: 20m Free Play/ Circuits - Lesson 63
lessons.body:
⍞ Materials: 303 Tween-approved Exercises + Active Games, a chair and ball for each child

➜ EXERCISE  
Circuit 14: Lower Body Training

1\. Warm-Up Activity (5 min)
(Pick an activity from p.24-25)

2\. Stretching Exercises (5 min)
(Pick 3 stretches from p.26-31)

3\. Circuit 2: Lower Body Training
• Abdominal Crunches (#75) 5x
• Pointer Reaches (#76) 5x
• Bottom Blaster (#131) 10x each 
• Contract and Extend (#132) 5x each
• Single-Leg Side Squats (#133) 5x each

4\. Add some kind of cardio exercise or sports game at some point throughout the day. There are many ideas in the book.
